page: Vultr voc-m-1c-8gb-50s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring a shared AMD EPYC-Rome processor with 1 vCPU and 1 physical core. Operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. Local storage is provided via a bundled 50 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mixed profile. The single-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% via stress-ng testing, though multi-core capabilities remain limited by the single-thread design. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 130.9 GB/sec and large block uncached bandwidth at 22.42 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 10%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels, registering 27.17 GB/sec and 11.19 GB/sec respectively.

From a resource density perspective, this instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for a shared-core configuration, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring more RAM than compute power. However, database operations and static web serving throughput are low, with Redis SET operations reaching 1.96 million operations per second. This server is best suited for lightweight memory-bound tasks, basic single-threaded applications, and small-scale development environments rather than high-throughput database or web serving workloads.
description: Vultr voc-m-1c-8gb-50s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance configured with 1 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPU, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 50 GB NVMe SSD. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server features a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, positioning it as a memory-optimized option within the shared-core tier. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, though memory bandwidth and multi-core metrics are weak. It is best suited for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads, single-threaded applications, and development environments.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-1c-8gb-50s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-1c-8gb-50s-amd is a shared AMD EPYC server with 1 vCPU, 8.0 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe SSD storage, designed for lightweight memory-bound tasks.
tagline: A shared-core AMD EPYC instance featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Single shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPU operating at 2.0 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory providing a high memory-to-core ratio
Bundled 50 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance but limited multi-core capacity
Best suited for lightweight memory-bound tasks and development environments
